text.title: file-reference-passing Failure Modes
text.summary: Five failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Stage 1 returns "summary AND content of relevant items".
antipattern.reason: Content duplicated across stages consumes tokens twice; the summary itself becomes context-debt the next stage must re-summarise.

section.p: Symptom: stage 1 output tokens grow linearly with corpus size. Root cause: content embedded in scan result. Fix: return refs only; stage 2 re-fetches on demand.

antipattern.description: Subagent returns the entire file content it analysed.
antipattern.reason: Parent context blows up, especially with multi-file subagents. The parent then re-summarises content it could have re-read on demand.

section.p: Symptom: parent context utilisation spikes after every subagent call. Root cause: subagent emits content. Fix: subagent schema must be `{summary, interesting_refs[], follow_up_questions[]}` — no content.

antipattern.description: Refs are LLM-generated paths that do not exist.
antipattern.reason: Downstream load fails or, worse, loads the wrong file. The pipeline silently degrades.

section.p: Symptom: downstream "file not found" errors; sometimes worse, wrong-file loads. Root cause: refs not constrained to a manifest. Fix: validate refs against the manifest after every scan; retry-with-feedback when invalid.

antipattern.description: Refs are remote URLs that expire.
antipattern.reason: Reruns days later fail; debugging is hard because the original content is gone.

section.p: Symptom: reruns of historical pipeline output fail. Root cause: live-URL refs. Fix: snapshot the resource at scan time; ref the snapshot path.

antipattern.description: Pipeline state files persist raw content between steps.
antipattern.reason: Disk bloats; state files become slow to load; the system cannot benefit from upstream refresh.

section.p: Symptom: state files in GB-range; cold-start latency unacceptable. Root cause: content persisted instead of refs. Fix: persist only refs; resolve at use time.
